sources.name: Damen product sheets (AHTS / PSV / CSOV / FCMV)
sources.format: PDF product sheets + web spec tables
sources.notes: OEM/builder authoritative. Per-model PDF product sheets (e.g. medialibrary.damen.com/.../product-sheet-ahts-180.pdf) give L/B/D, deadweight, deck area, deck load, bollard pull, tank capacities, crane SWL/outreach, accommodation. No RAOs or full mass-property (GM/radii of gyration) tables. Best single starting point for support-fleet principal particulars + deck/crane.

sources.name: Ulstein ship-design vessel pages (SX-series CSOV/SOV)
sources.notes: Designer-authoritative for walk-to-work CSOV/SOV. Gives LOA/beam, accommodation (POB), deck area, motion-compensated crane SWL, gangway, DP class, and stated operability Hs for gangway/crane (e.g. SX250 'up to ~2 m Hs'). Useful directly for SOV operability limits. No mass-property or RAO datasets.

sources.name: VARD / other OSV builder vessel pages (Vard, Havyard, Salt Ship Design, Kongsberg-Maritime designs)
sources.format: web spec pages + occasional brochure PDFs
sources.notes: Builder/designer-authoritative for PSV/AHTS/CSOV. Comparable layer coverage to Ulstein. Aggregate several builders to widen fleet coverage. No RAO/mass-property.

sources.name: Damen / OEM PSV & AHTS brochure PDFs mirrored on Scribd
sources.notes: Convenient mirrors of builder PSV/AHTS series brochures with full spec tables, but Scribd is registration/credit-walled and versions may be outdated. Prefer pulling the same sheet from the builder's own medialibrary when available; use Scribd only as fallback / for retired models.

sources.name: GRS Offshore Renewables 3D vessel portfolio (W2W/SOV)
sources.notes: Shipbroker portfolio of real in-service W2W/SOV/CSOV units with summary particulars, crane and gangway data. Good for as-built fleet inventory cross-check; figures are marketing-grade, verify against builder sheets.

sources.name: Offshore Supply & Support Vessels - World Wide directory (Refvik.info)
sources.notes: Free fleet directory: year built, GT, deadweight, bhp/power, bollard pull, class per vessel across the global OSV fleet. Broad but shallow (no deck/crane curves, no RAO) and several years old. Good for breadth + as-built power/BP; treat as a free analogue to the paid registers.

sources.name: DNV-RP-C205 - Environmental conditions and environmental loads
sources.format: landing page (standard itself is purchase/registration; older 2019 edition PDF circulates on third-party mirrors)
sources.notes: The authoritative methodology RP for metocean characterization (short-term & long-term wave/wind/current models, joint distributions, design sea states) underpinning operability-limit assessment. Document is purchase-gated; the landing page + methodology summary are public. Pair with measured/hindcast data sources for actual site numbers.

sources.name: DNV-ST-N001 / DNVGL-ST-0111 marine-operations & SOV standards
sources.notes: Marine-operations weather-window and SOV/W2W operability-limit framing (alpha-factors, operational vs design limits). Methodology public via summaries/webinars; full text paywalled. Relevant for converting metocean stats into vessel operability limits.

sources.name: BOEM Metocean Characterization Recommended Practices for U.S. Offshore Wind
sources.format: PDF (3.7 MB, free download, confirmed live)
sources.notes: U.S. government recommended practice: wind, wave (Hs/Tp/spectra), current, water level, extreme & normal conditions, measurement and hindcast guidance for US OSW. Free and authoritative. Region-specific to US OSW lease areas; best public metocean-criteria reference for the support-vessel operability use case.

sources.name: Copernicus Marine Service (CMEMS) wave/wind/current hindcast & reanalysis
sources.notes: Free (account required) gridded multi-year wave (Hs, Tp, direction), wind and current products incl. regional reanalyses (e.g. Arctic, global). Source of the actual site-specific metocean statistics behind operability/weather-window analysis. Data only - no vessel specs.

sources.name: NOAA National Data Buoy Center (NDBC)
sources.notes: Free in-situ buoy observations (Hs, dominant/avg period, wind, met) for US waters incl. GoM and US east/west coast. Ground-truth for hindcasts and direct operability-limit calibration. US coverage only.

sources.name: Norwegian hindcast metocean (NORA3 / NORA-SARAH, MET Norway)
sources.notes: Free high-resolution North Sea / Norwegian Sea wind-wave hindcast (NORA3) widely used for North Sea offshore-wind & OSV operability. Open framework (NORA-SARAH) for downscaled coastal hydrodynamics. Regional (NE Atlantic / North Sea). Data only.

sources.name: Orcina OrcaFlex documentation - vessel RAO data handling
sources.notes: Not a data source for vessel RAOs but the authoritative reference on RAO conventions/formats (displacement RAOs, phase conventions, import formats). RAOs themselves are vessel-confidential and generally NOT public - see paywalled_gaps. Useful for the schema/format of the RAO layer.

paywalled_gaps: Motion RAOs for specific support vessels (AHTS/PSV/CTV/SOV): essentially NOT publicly available. RAOs are computed per-hull (model basin or diffraction codes WAMIT/AQWA/HydD) and held by builders/owners/class as confidential design data. Public domain has only academic examples (FPSO/ship RAOs in ScienceDirect/ResearchGate/DTIC/TU Delft papers) and tool docs (Orcina/Orcaflex). For a real database you must generate RAOs from hull geometry or obtain them under NDA from builder/owner.

Mass properties (lightship weight distribution, VCG/LCG, GM, radii of gyration / inertia): not published in builder brochures; come from the vessel's stability book / inclining test, which is class- and owner-confidential. Public sources give only displacement/deadweight, not the inertia tensor needed for seakeeping.

Crane capacity load charts (full SWL vs radius curves, dynamic/offboard de-rating): brochures give headline SWL and sometimes one radius; complete capacity curves live in OEM crane manuals (MacGregor, Liebherr, Palfinger, Huisman) which are document-controlled / not freely downloadable.

IHS Markit / S&P Global Petrodata (ODS-Petrodata, RigPoint, Offshore Rig/Vessel data): paid subscription. Authoritative for OSV/rig fleet, ownership, day-rates, utilization, principal particulars - but fully paywalled.

Clarksons Research - World Fleet Register / World Offshore Register (WOR) / Offshore Intelligence Network: paid subscription. Most comprehensive offshore-vessel principal-particulars + market dataset; bespoke data also paid.
Enverus (formerly Drillinginfo, includes RS Energy/offshore intel): paid subscription; offshore market & asset intelligence, not freely accessible.

Regional design-level metocean criteria (extreme Hs/Tp return-period values) baked into API RP 2MET / API RP 2A-WSD Sec.17 and ISO 19901-1: the standards are purchase-gated; only summaries and OTC papers are free. Site-specific design metocean reports from consultancies (Fugro, ABPmer, BMT) are commissioned/paid.
